title: Perfect N-Queens Backtracking Bug Fix: Duplicate Solution Detection

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a single integer representing the size of the board (n).
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a boolean indicating whether a perfect N-Queens solution exists.

examples.explanation: There exists a perfect N-Queens solution for a 4x4 chessboard.
There does not exist a perfect N-Queens solution for a 3x3 chessboard.

hints.text: Consider using a flag or a counter to track duplicate solutions.

editorial.title: Editorial: Duplicate Solution Detection in N-Queens
editorial.intuition: Use backtracking to explore possible board configurations and keep track of duplicate solutions.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Try all possible board configurations and count the number of duplicate solutions.
editorial.betterSolution: Improve the brute force approach by using a hash set to store unique solutions.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Use a single flag to track duplicate solutions and prune the search space efficiently.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution is correct because it efficiently detects duplicate solutions and explores the search space without repetition.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Try a small input (e.g., n=4) and manually verify the output.
